payload.text: Independent review C3 (Critical, confirmed live): cmd_premise --supersedes refuses only live/unverified old premises, so a RETRACTED premise can be redirected to a fresh claim -- with NO human gate on the verb. An agent (no TRUTH_HUMAN) redirected a human-retracted P0 premise and RELEASED the blocked work while the claim stayed terminal. Paper section 1 says retraction cannot be undone by any later event; ADR-013 lists retracted among supersede-eligible dead states -> cross-surface contradiction on the headline property, defended at the status layer, spent at the readiness layer. Fix (ADR-017): superseding a retracted premise requires the ADR-011 human gate (TRUTH_HUMAN + typed-id ack), symmetric with the authority that imposed the retraction; mechanical dead states (stale/diverged/cannot_verify/missing) stay ungated. Amends ADR-013 intake. Canary + core test; paper section-1 terminality clarified.
